title: perula benghalensis
intro: Perula benghalensis, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Perula benghalensis include the relief of symptoms associated with fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential in lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Perula benghalensis are typically made by infusing its roots or leaves in hot water, or by using it in combination with other herbs in decoctions or tonics.
